content: Just as the linguistic teaching definition of reflexion frames it as both a physical reflection of light/sound and an intentional cognitive act of reviewing learning, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to separate two core functions: a passive 'reflection' step that mirrors inconsistencies in the model's own outputs, and an active 'reflective review' step that deliberately edits erroneous reasoning paths aligned with learning objectives, borrowing the dual definition of the term itself to resolve current limitations of single-perspective self-reflection. This dual functional split draws directly from the dual meaning of reflexion that appears in both general linguistic definition and AI agent research to create a new structured self-reflection framework.
